strategy_logic: This breakout strategy buys XLE when the closing price finishes above the highest high from the prior 20 trading days. Trades are entered at the next day's open, risk is capped with a stop-loss set 2.0 ATR below the entry price, profits are taken at 2.0 ATR above the entry price, and any position still open after 15 trading days is closed with a market order at the next open.
asset_selection: I screened the ETF universe SPY, QQQ, IWM, XLE, GLD, TLT using a rolling walk-forward test with one year of training followed by one quarter of out-of-sample testing. XLE was selected because it delivered the strongest out-of-sample Sharpe ratio in the screen. The top three assets by walk-forward Sharpe were XLE, QQQ, IWM. The historical data loader tries ShinyBroker first; when no local IBKR/TWS session is running, it falls back to a public Yahoo chart endpoint.
breakout_definition: A breakout occurs when today's close is greater than the highest high from the previous 20 sessions. The implementation uses a shifted rolling maximum to avoid look-ahead bias. ATR uses a 14-day window. The stop-loss threshold is entry price minus 2.0 ATR, the profit target is entry price plus 2.0 ATR, and the timeout rule closes any remaining position after 15 trading days.
results_summary: The stitched out-of-sample backtest on XLE produced 16 trades with an average return per trade of -0.59%, an annualized Sharpe ratio of 0.35, and a maximum drawdown of -10.49%. All metrics assume a 2.0% annual risk-free rate.
